No Poverty.reason: Analyzes 'Political and Economic Governance' and 'economic development,' which are foundational to creating pro-poor policies and reducing poverty as per Targets 1.b and 1.2.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: Focuses on 'economic development' and 'African Political Economy,' directly analyzing policies and structures that impact economic growth and productivity (Targets 8.1, 8.2, 8.3).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The study of 'economic development' and 'African Political Economy' may inferentially include discussions on infrastructure and industrialization (Targets 9.1, 9.2), but it is not a focus.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: Directly analyzes drivers of inequality like 'Ethnicity and Identity' and 'Social Classes,' linking to the promotion of social and political inclusion (Targets 10.2, 10.3).

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course is a core study of this goal, covering 'Military in Politics,' 'Political and Economic Governance,' 'Post-Conflict Societies,' and 'Transitional Justice' (Targets 16.1, 16.3, 16.6).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: Explicitly analyzes 'Africa's International Political Relations' and 'Economic Relations,' including 'Neo-Colonialism,' which directly relate to global partnerships and policy coherence (Targets 17.10, 17.14).
